title: Robust System — Error Handling
description: Add exception handling and templates to make the inventory system robust. Wrap user input in try/catch blocks to handle invalid data (e.g., non-numeric price). Create a template function that can sort products by any field (price, name, quantity). Use throw to raise custom exceptions for errors like 'product not found' or 'inventory full'. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.
test_code:
assert 'try' in cpp, 'Use try blocks to handle potential errors'
assert 'catch' in cpp, 'Use catch blocks to handle exceptions'
assert 'throw' in cpp, 'Use throw to raise exceptions for error conditions'
assert 'template' in cpp, 'Write a template function for flexible sorting'
assert 'runtime_error' in cpp or 'invalid_argument' in cpp or 'exception' in cpp, 'Use standard exception types (runtime_error, invalid_argument, etc.)'
assert 'stdexcept' in cpp or 'exception' in cpp, 'Include <stdexcept> for standard exception classes'
assert 'sort' in cpp, 'Use std::sort or a custom sort with the template function'
assert cpp.count('catch') >= 1, 'Include at least one catch block for error handling'
assert cpp.count('throw') >= 2, 'Throw exceptions for at least two different error conditions (e.g., full inventory, invalid input)'
acceptance_criteria: try/catch blocks wrap user input handling in main
throw used for at least two error conditions (full inventory, invalid input, product not found)
Template function defined for sorting products by different fields
Uses std::sort or custom sort with the template comparator
Includes <stdexcept> and uses standard exception types
Program gracefully handles errors instead of crashing
hints: template <typename Compare> void sortProducts(vector<Product>& v, Compare comp) { sort(v.begin(), v.end(), comp); }
Call it with a lambda: sortProducts(products, [](const Product& a, const Product& b) { return a.price < b.price; })
Use throw runtime_error("message") to raise exceptions
Catch with: catch (const runtime_error& e) { cout << "Error: " << e.what() << endl; }
Wrap cin >> in a try block and check cin.fail() to detect non-numeric input
